_publications._publicationTitle: Transient inhibition of the ERK pathway prevents cerebellar developmental defects and improves long-term motor functions in murine models of neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: Of 14 MEKi-treated Nf1 hGFAP CKO cerebella analyzed, 9 mice (good-responders or G-responders) exhibited a significant rescue... However, the remaining 5 of the MEKi-treated Nf1 hGFAP CKO mice (poor-responders or P-responders) only exhibited marginal improvement
details: At 5 mg/kg dose, 9 out of 14 MEKi-treated Nf1 hGFAP CKO mice showed significant rescue (G-responders), while 5 showed marginal improvement (P-responders)
